title: Competitive Exam Practice Questions
description: Comprehensive collection of practice questions for SSC and other competitive exams

questions.question: A student scored 85% marks. If he scored 425 marks, what were the total marks?
questions.explanation: 85% of total marks = 425, so total marks = 425 × 100/85 = 500

questions.question: The price of an article is increased by 20% and then decreased by 20%. What is the net effect?
questions.explanation: Let original price = 100. After 20% increase = 120. After 20% decrease = 120 × 0.8 = 96. Net decrease = 4%

questions.question: If A's salary is 25% more than B's salary, then B's salary is how much percent less than A's salary?
questions.explanation: Let B's salary = 100, then A's salary = 125. B's salary is 25 less than A's, which is 25/125 × 100 = 20%

questions.question: In an examination, 35% of the students failed and 420 students passed. How many students appeared for the examination?
A shopkeeper buys an article for ₹400 and sells it for ₹500. What is his profit percentage?
A man sells an article at a loss of 10%. If he sells it for ₹90 more, he would gain 10%. What is the cost price?
questions.explanation: Let CP = x. SP at 10% loss = 0.9x. SP at 10% gain = 1.1x. Difference = 0.2x = 90, so x = 450

questions.question: A shopkeeper offers a discount of 20% and still makes a profit of 20%. What is the marked price if the cost price is ₹100?
The simple interest on a sum for 5 years at 8% per annum is ₹600. What is the compound interest on the same sum for 2 years at 10% per annum?
A sum of money doubles itself in 10 years at simple interest. What is the rate of interest?
questions.explanation: If sum doubles, interest = principal. P = P × R × 10/100, so R = 10%

questions.question: The difference between compound interest and simple interest on a sum for 2 years at 8% per annum is ₹64. What is the sum?
If A:B = 3:4 and B:C = 5:6, then A:C = ?
Divide ₹900 among A, B, C in the ratio 2:3:4. What is B's share?
The ratio of boys to girls in a school is 3:5. If there are 600 girls, what is the total number of students?
questions.explanation: Boys:Girls = 3:5. If girls = 600, then boys = (3/5) × 600 = 360. Total = 600 + 360 = 960

questions.question: A can do a work in 15 days and B can do it in 20 days. How long will they take to complete the work together?
questions.explanation: A's 1 day work = 1/15, B's 1 day work = 1/20. Together = 1/15 + 1/20 = 7/60. Time = 60/7 = 8.57 days

questions.question: A and B together can complete a work in 12 days. A alone can do it in 20 days. How long will B take to complete the work alone?
questions.explanation: A+B's 1 day work = 1/12, A's 1 day work = 1/20. B's 1 day work = 1/12 - 1/20 = 2/60 = 1/30. So B takes 30 days

questions.question: 3 men or 4 women can complete a work in 43 days. How long will 7 men and 5 women take to complete the work?
questions.explanation: 3 men = 4 women, so 1 man = 4/3 women. 7 men + 5 women = 7×(4/3) + 5 = 28/3 + 5 = 43/3 women. Time = 43 ÷ (43/3) = 3 days. Wait, this seems incorrect. Let me recalculate: 3 men = 4 women, so 1 woman = 3/4 men. 7 men + 5 women = 7 + 5×(3/4) = 7 + 15/4 = 43/4 men. Time = 43 ÷ (43/4) = 4 days. Actually, let me use the correct approach: 3 men take 43 days, so 1 man takes 129 days. 7 men + 5 women = 7 + 5×(3/4) = 7 + 3.75 = 10.75 men equivalent. Time = 129 ÷ 10.75 = 12 days

questions.question: A train travels 180 km in 2 hours. What is its speed?
A car travels at 60 km/h for the first 2 hours and at 80 km/h for the next 3 hours. What is the average speed?
questions.explanation: Total distance = 60×2 + 80×3 = 120 + 240 = 360 km. Total time = 5 hours. Average speed = 360/5 = 72 km/h

questions.question: A train 150m long crosses a platform 100m long in 10 seconds. What is the speed of the train?
questions.explanation: Total distance = 150 + 100 = 250m. Time = 10s. Speed = 250/10 = 25 m/s = 90 km/h

questions.question: If x + 1/x = 3, then x² + 1/x² = ?
If a + b = 5 and ab = 6, then a³ + b³ = ?
Simplify: (2x + 3)² - (2x - 3)²
The area of a square is 144 cm². What is the length of its diagonal?
The radius of a circle is 7 cm. What is its area? (π = 22/7)
A triangle has sides 3cm, 4cm, 5cm. What is its area?
questions.explanation: It's a right triangle (3² + 4² = 5²). Area = (1/2) × 3 × 4 = 6 cm²

questions.question: What is the HCF of 36 and 48?
What is the LCM of 15 and 20?
Which of the following is divisible by 11?
How many ways can 5 people be arranged in a row?
How many ways can a committee of 3 be selected from 8 people?
How many 3-digit numbers can be formed using digits 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 if repetition is allowed?
questions.explanation: Each digit has 5 choices, so total = 5 × 5 × 5 = 125

questions.question: A coin is tossed 3 times. What is the probability of getting exactly 2 heads?
questions.explanation: Total outcomes = 2³ = 8. Favorable outcomes = HHT, HTH, THH = 3. P = 3/8

questions.question: A die is rolled. What is the probability of getting a prime number?
questions.explanation: Prime numbers on a die: 2, 3, 5 (3 outcomes). P = 3/6 = 1/2

questions.question: Two cards are drawn from a deck of 52 cards. What is the probability that both are kings?
